input.programNotes:
Programming Mode: Auto
Planning Style: Collaborative Mix
Validation Source: user-uploaded office plan image plan.jpg. The image visibly includes meeting rooms, reception, staff canteen, open office areas, computer facilities, storage, manager offices, restrooms/core/service rooms, perimeter glazing, entries/doors, and interior partitions.

reviewReasons: Uploaded plan content has not yet been machine-extracted in this V1 sandbox path; shell, core, entries, glazing, and walls are inferred placeholders requiring confirmation.
Do not treat default core/restroom/stair/elevator positions as confirmed existing conditions until the uploaded plan is reviewed.
Parser confidence is below the architectural acceptance threshold, so scenario outputs must remain needs-review until shell/core confirmation is completed.

existingInventory.summary: Existing conditions indicate approximately 85 reusable workstation seats, 7 private offices, 4 meeting rooms, fixed core elements, and interior zones that can be selectively reconfigured.

reusableZones.notes: Likely reusable with finish refresh and furniture updates.
Meeting rooms can generally remain if technology and finishes are upgraded.
Wet-wall adjacency makes this a strong reuse candidate.

repurposableZones.notes: Systems furniture is estimated at the current 50 SF planning target, with 36–64 SF treated as the acceptable workstation range.
Can be repurposed as focus, lounge, or informal collaboration space.

fixedElements.notes: Preserve shell, core, restrooms, stairs, elevators, and shafts in every scenario.

ambiguousAreas.approximateLocation: Uploaded plan underlay / inferred tenant area
ambiguousAreas.notes: Shell/core confirmation is recommended before treating this as an as-built test-fit.

reconfigurationZones.notes: Candidate walls for selective demolition or reconfiguration in Moderate Build-Out.

reconfigurationZones.approximateLocation: Interior tenant improvement area outside fixed core
reconfigurationZones.notes: Full Transformation assumes these flexible zones can be rebuilt around the preserved shell/core.

scenarios.label: Light Refresh
scenarios.layoutDescription: This scenario leverages the existing layout, focusing on cosmetic updates and furniture. It prioritizes speed to occupancy and cost savings by retaining most partitions and infrastructure. The layout will feel familiar, with updated aesthetics to reflect a modern technology/professional services environment. Lower budget and shorter schedule reflect high reuse and minimal demolition.

schedulePhases.description: Updated furniture layout, finish selections, and minor partition modifications. Minimal permit requirements.
Remove existing finishes, flooring, and fixtures only. No structural or MEP demolition required.
New flooring, paint, lighting upgrades, and minor millwork. Reuse all existing walls and infrastructure.
Furniture installation, minor IT/AV updates, and signage.
Final walk-through, punch list items, and occupancy.

scenarios.aiSummary: This 'Light Refresh' scenario is ideal for the tenant seeking rapid occupancy and minimal upfront costs, utilizing 75% of the space efficiently by largely preserving the existing floor plan. While it may not fully meet the requested program's room counts, it offers a functional space with new finishes, flooring, and furniture. This option presents the lowest cost and shortest timeline, allowing the tenant to quickly establish a presence in the New York market with a refreshed, yet largely pre-determined, layout. Light Refresh achieves approximately 22% of the requested program while minimizing demolition, new partitions, and schedule impact. Remaining differences are fit variance, not a software failure.

scenarios.label: Moderate Build-Out
scenarios.layoutDescription: This plan fully aligns with the tenant's program, incorporating selective demolition and new construction to optimize the space. The layout balances open collaborative areas with sufficient private and meeting spaces. It reconfigures parts of the core to enhance flow and functionality, creating a modern and efficient environment. Mid-range budget and schedule reflect selective demolition, retained core infrastructure, and targeted build-back.

scenarios.aiSummary: The 'Moderate Build-Out' scenario perfectly matches the tenant's program requirements, achieving an 82% efficiency score through strategic reconfigurations. This approach involves selective demolition and new construction, allowing for a tailored layout that optimizes both collaborative and private work zones. It represents a balanced investment in cost and time, providing a highly functional and customized space without a full gut renovation. This scenario offers the tenant a well-designed office that supports their collaborative culture and growth needs effectively. Moderate Build-Out achieves approximately 27% of the requested program through targeted reconfiguration and selective reuse of existing conditions.

scenarios.layoutDescription: This scenario involves near-complete demolition of interior partitions, creating a blank canvas for optimal space planning. The layout is fully customized to maximize natural light, flow, and program efficiency. It delivers a premium, future-proofed environment tailored precisely to the tenant's brand and operational needs, with an emphasis on high-quality finishes and integrated technology. Highest budget and longest schedule reflect full interior redesign while preserving shell and fixed core elements.

schedulePhases.description: Complete architectural and engineering drawings, MEP design, structural review, and full permit package. Landlord approval required.
Complete gut demolition — all walls, ceilings, flooring, and MEP systems removed to base building condition.
New mechanical, electrical, and plumbing systems installed from scratch. Includes HVAC, sprinkler, and data infrastructure.
Framing, drywall, specialty finishes, custom millwork, and all architectural elements.
Full furniture package, comprehensive technology infrastructure, audio-visual systems, and signage program.
Final inspections, commissioning, certificate of occupancy, and phased move-in.

scenarios.aiSummary: The 'Full Transformation' scenario provides the ultimate long-term solution, achieving an 88% efficiency score by completely optimizing the space for the tenant's program and future growth. This approach involves extensive demolition and a full build-out, ensuring a bespoke design that perfectly reflects the tenant's brand and collaborative culture. While it represents the highest cost and longest timeline, it yields the best long-term outcome, offering a state-of-the-art office environment. This option allows the tenant to create a truly distinctive and highly functional space in the competitive New York market. Full Transformation achieves approximately 97% of the requested program by preserving shell/core and redesigning flexible interior zones for best long-term fit.
